title: Dress for the Weather
summary: Choose clothing appropriate for the current or expected weather conditions.
learnerPromise: You will be able to pick clothing that keeps you comfortable and safe in common weather conditions.
whyItMatters: Wearing the right clothing for the weather helps you stay comfortable, avoid getting too hot or too cold, and enjoy your time outside more.
realLifeUses: Choosing layers for a cold morning that warms up later
Wearing breathable clothing on a hot day to stay cool
Picking waterproof shoes or a rain jacket when it is wet outside
Adding a hat and gloves when the temperature drops below freezing
materialsNeeded: Clothing options for the expected weather
result: An outfit chosen to match the current or expected weather.
youWillLearn: How to dress in layers for changing temperatures
How to choose fabrics that breathe or insulate
How to add rain, wind, or sun protection when needed
How to adjust your outfit when the weather changes unexpectedly
miniChallenge: Look at today's weather and pick an outfit that matches it. Check that you have one item for warmth, one for comfort, and one for protection if needed.
steps: Check the temperature, rain chance, and wind for the day.
Choose a base layer that matches the main temperature.
Add a mid-layer or outer layer if it will be cold or windy.
Include rain protection if showers are likely.
Do a quick comfort check in the mirror before leaving.
completionCriteria: You chose an outfit based on the day's weather.
You felt comfortable outside for most of the day.
commonProblems: Wearing too many layers and overheating
Forgetting rain protection when the chance of rain is low but real
Choosing style over comfort and being too cold or too hot
tips: Layers are easier to adjust than one heavy coat.
Cotton holds moisture, so choose synthetic or wool layers for active or wet days.
Keep a light jacket or umbrella in your bag during changing seasons.
If you are unsure, step outside for a moment before finalizing your choice.
